node.text:
With a deep understanding of the vibrant community of Naples, our bankers can help you craft a multigenerational wealth plan to achieve your long-term goals. Our experienced team—including specialists in family governance, philanthropy and beyond—can help you make an impact now and into the future.

Learn more about our Naples team by tapping the link in bio.

As we step into 2025, the financial landscape is both challenging and full of opportunity. Our Global Investment Strategy Group has crafted three resolutions to guide your investment journey. Read this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways to learn more about navigating the year ahead with confidence.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Just released: Eye on the Market Outlook 2025. 

With questions about deregulation, deportations, tariffs, tax cuts, cost cutting…what could possibly go wrong? Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy Michael Cembalest guides us through the 2025 investment landscape, from AI to the nuclear renaissance, to crypto and beyond—and shares his Top Ten list for the new year. #jpmorganprivatebank 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text: As we reach the close of 2024, we’d like to share a few of our favorite moments. It was a year full of meaningful connections and amazing experiences that spanned the globe. We cannot wait to continue this incredible journey with you in 2025.

node.text:
In 1914, artist Frederick Kreig completed a series of mosaics commissioned by the late J. Pierpont Morgan to adorn the firm’s headquarters at 23 Wall Street in New York City. This panel, representing knowledge, stood beside others representing commerce, industry, the arts, recreation and more. Now preserved in the JPMorganChase Corporate History Collection, this artwork and its themes still resonate with us over a century later.

To those near and far, we send you warm wishes for the holidays and a happy new year.

node.text:
Meet the talented bankers in our Salt Lake City office, serving clients throughout Utah and Idaho. Working as an integrated team, our specialists can partner with you to achieve your unique goals. With a long history serving the Utah region, we’re committed to helping clients explore new opportunities and make the most of their wealth. 

Click the link in bio to learn more.

This week, the Federal Reserve cut its policy target range by 25 basis points, bringing the target rate to 4.25%–4.5%. This marks 100 basis points of cuts in the current rate-normalizing cycle, which began in September.

The Fed’s projections for interest rates have been revised upward, indicating a more gradual pace of interest rate normalization. The move signals the Fed is more comfortable with the current level of rates to support a robust economy. 

Our view is that policy will continue to normalize (move lower) this cycle. The Fed is providing support to the labor market and is confident the disinflationary process has not stalled.

We got the answers to many longstanding questions in 2024 about the economy, the U.S. presidency, the S&P 500 and more. But as we approach the final weeks of the year, there are a few things still on our minds. Discover what investors want to know about China’s growth, the global easing cycle and tariffs in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Since 2023, the “Magnificent 7” have been the stars of the stock market, driving over half of the S&P 500’s impressive 62% return. Their earnings surged by 40%, while the rest of the market, the “Remaining 493,” saw a modest 2% growth. However, we anticipate changes on the horizon.

As we look to 2025, the earnings landscape is expected to shift. The Remaining 493 are projected to boost their earnings growth to 13%*, more than five times their recent performance. This broadening of growth is fueled by factors such as lower interest rates and a return to normal production levels.

Moving into 2025, this shift suggests a more balanced market and could offer a more diverse opportunity set for investors.

*Earnings projections are from Factset.

node.text:
Welcome to Miami and the final installment of our 2024 Emerging Family Leaders series. With discussions on AI, wellness, leadership and more, we are closing out the year with new inspiration and energy.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We have entered the last month of the year, and U.S. equities continue to outperform.

After boasting 24% returns in 2023, U.S. equities show no signs of slowing down in 2024. The S&P 500, which tracks large-cap U.S. equity performance, is up over 26% year-to-date, compared to world equities excluding the United States, which are up approximately 5%. These numbers suggest that U.S. exceptionalism is still present, and we believe this trend can continue into 2025.

Encouragingly, when we look deeper, returns are broadening in the United States. The “Magnificent 7” have accounted for about 44% of the index returns so far in 2024, compared to 60% in 2023. In other words, the other 493 stocks are contributing more significantly, and we believe this increase can also continue in the coming year.

As Naples grows and evolves, we're excited to be part of its transformation, supporting both long-time residents and newcomers who are shaping the city’s future. Whether you’re looking to preserve your legacy or pursue new opportunities, we’re here to guide you through every step of your financial journey with tailored advice and personalized service. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how our Naples team can help you.

In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we’re looking back at the last 11 months in markets to reveal what we’re most grateful for this season. Like any year, 2024 had its ups and downs—but we’re heading into the holidays with optimism. 

Tap the link in bio to learn why.

At the Private Bank, we recognize the remarkable growth happening across Arizona. Bankers Nate Willardson and Evan Kurtz, alongside Investment Specialist Holly Rezzonico, share their excitement to be part of this transformation, and reflect on how their deep ties to the region make their work especially meaningful. From guiding clients through complex financial scenarios to investing in the state’s vibrant growth, our commitment runs deep. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about how our Phoenix team can help you.

node.text: Discover a world of inspiration with J.P. Morgan’s #NextList2025. From vibrant art galleries in Norway and India to books by legendary leaders and bold Shakespeare revivals, NextList 2025 showcases the must-reads and the must-see experiences that will captivate you this holiday season and beyond.

On the heels of launching our 2025 Outlook, we’re taking a moment to reflect on the markets’ past, present and future in #TopMarketTakeaways. Investors faced many worries in 2024—about inflation, the U.S. elections, geopolitics and beyond—but portfolios are closing out the year in a position of strength. 

Before carving your turkey, tap the link in bio to learn what we think will be top of mind for investors in 2025.

It’s exciting times for AI and investment. While U.S. big tech is ramping up AI spending, we’re just at the start of this transformative journey. AI models are advancing rapidly, and costs are dropping significantly, making AI more accessible.

Corporate investment is currently modest, but there’s room for growth as AI use cases become clearer. Imagine AI lawyers, software engineers and even investment strategists revolutionizing the services sector. Companies that integrate AI effectively could see enhanced productivity and cost-efficiency.

While challenges such as regulation exist, the investment opportunities in AI are promising. Just as the cloud and mobile transitions birthed corporate giants, AI could foster a similar ecosystem.

To read more about #Outlook2025 tap the link in bio.

#jpmorganprivatebank

We’re excited to share our Annual Outlook: Building on Strength, which highlights the resilience and opportunities within the global economy. From rate policy and AI advancements to accelerating capital investments, dive into the key themes that we think will drive growth in 2025.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

#Outlook2025 #jpmorganprivatebank

We’re 50 days away from the new year—and only two days from launching our Outlook for 2025. In the meantime, we’re looking back at the last six months. How did the views from our 2024 Mid-Year Outlook play out? From higher rates to artificial intelligence to the U.S. deficit, we take an honest look in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

At vibrant gatherings across New York, Palo Alto, Bellevue, Chicago, Houston and Dallas, our Asian Wealth Strategies team brought together clients, families and friends to celebrate Diwali, the festival of lights. With diyas lit, we reflected on the bonds that connect us and the strong relationships that bring us together.

node.text: Join us as we experience the charms of Paris, from the captivating exhibitions at Paris Photo to a gathering of today’s visionaries at our next-generation event. The week has been a whirlwind of inspiration, networking and fresh perspectives, all set against the breathtaking backdrop of the City of Light.

The third edition of our annual summit for the next generation was a vibrant confluence of discovery and connection, bringing together young leaders, clients and guests to engage with visionaries from diverse backgrounds. Over two days, we hosted insightful discussions on topics such as entrepreneurship, business development and management, technology and more, curated to resonate with the interests of emerging leaders. This memorable gathering was further enriched by the art and culture of Paris, with highlights including a sunset cruise on the Seine and an exclusive preview at Paris Photo.

During what was a very busy week in the United States, the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points to a range of 4.5%–4.75% at its November meeting. This unanimous decision among voting members follows a previous 50-basis-point cut in September. After the meeting, Chair Jerome Powell emphasized that the risks to the Fed’s dual mandate—maximum employment and stable prices—are balanced. The rate cut aims to sustain labor market strength while continuing to make progress on inflation, which is on a sustainable downward path. Despite a smaller move compared to September, Powell said that monetary policy remains restrictive, aligning with the Fed’s goal of achieving a soft economic landing.

For investors, this continuation of the easing cycle may present both challenges and opportunities. For starters, reinvestment risk is real—and bond yields could move lower from here. But it’s not too late: Over the last 12 cutting cycles (from first cut to last cut), fixed income has outperformed cash in all but one. To boot, when the Fed cuts rates outside of a recession, equities have delivered a strong performance. 

Bonds are subject to interest rate, credit, and default risks. Equity prices can rise or fall due to market or company changes, sometimes unpredictably. Both bonds and equities carry risks, including potential loss of principal.
This #VeteransDay, we pay tribute to the brave individuals who have served our country with unwavering dedication. Join us in honoring their courage and commitment.
This #VeteransDay, we pay tribute to the brave individuals who have served our country with unwavering dedication. Join us in honoring their courage and commitment.

The easing cycle continues. This week, the Federal Reserve lowered its target interest range by 25 basis points to 4.5%–4.75%. How might this impact investors? While the U.S. election has certainly dominated headlines this week, we think the Fed’s moves and corporate earnings are ultimately more important for financial asset returns. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

Celebrating over 150 years of J.P. Morgan’s connection to Paris, we hosted a series of bespoke events. Guests enjoyed a unique experience at the Paris Photo Fair, under the Eiffel Tower, as we marked J.P. Morgan Private Bank's 13th year as proud sponsors.

What does the upcoming U.S. election mean for your portfolio? While volatility is elevated, election outcomes don’t typically drive market returns over the long run, so don’t let them derail your plans. With that said, investors are still asking important questions—about tax policy, government debt and deficit, and other key issues—which we try to answer in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The 2024 U.S. presidential election is less than a week away. As the big day approaches, investors might be wondering if election-related volatility could have a negative impact on their portfolios. However, history may provide a reassuring perspective.

Since 1960, the S&P 500 rallied an average of 4.6% between Election Day and year-end in 11 out of 16 elections. Five elections did see weaker equity markets following Election Day, with two deep sell-offs in 2000 and 2008, but those declines were largely because the economy was either in or on the verge of a recession. We think today’s economy is on solid footing. 

The bottom line? While volatility may be elevated leading up to and immediately following Election Day, it is crucial to maintain a long-term perspective. Historical data shows that election outcomes do not typically drive long-run market performance and alone should not derail your investment plan.

At the Private Bank, it’s not just about offering financial advice—it’s about building deep and lasting relationships with our clients. Bankers Andrea Casavilca and Mohamed Zeitoun share how we blend local insight with global resources to help meet unique and evolving client needs. Our commitment is to understand those we serve and deliver personalized solutions, supported by the full strength of J.P. Morgan. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more about our Phoenix team.

It’s easy to get caught up in the noise of daily financial headlines. Once in a while, it can be helpful to step back to examine the bigger picture, and recalibrate your portfolio accordingly. This week in #TopMarketTakeaways, we’re introducing the newly released 29th Long-Term Capital Market Assumptions. 

Discover why we’ve lowered return expectations, and where we see opportunity. Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We were delighted to be joined by Carlton McCoy, Jr., Chief Executive Officer of Lawrence Wine Estates, for our recent Black Wealth Strategies dinner in New York. One of the youngest ever to be named Master Sommelier, Carlton now runs the leading portfolio of historical fine wine estates and curated a selection of wines for the evening. He was joined by Private Bank’s Head of Black Wealth Strategies, Debra Langford, and Asset & Wealth Management's Chief Communications Officer, Darin Oduyoye, for a conversation on his storied career in the wine industry.

The economy has defied expectations of a slowdown this year. Currently, the Atlanta Fed GDPNow tracker (a real-time economic model that provides an estimate of the U.S. gross domestic product [GDP] growth rate for the current quarter) is estimating the U.S. economy to grow 3.4% this quarter.
 
The continued resilience of the economy has caused traders to pare back their expectations of interest rate cuts. Traders now expect the Federal Reserve will cut at a more gradual pace, which would imply a “soft landing,” a.k.a. avoiding a recession. We agree, and don’t see the need for a rapid easing of conditions from the Fed. Rather, we think a slower pace of interest rate cuts can be enough to help support the labor market, without knocking the disinflationary trend off-kilter.

JPMorganChase is proud to support the Academy Museum of Motion Pictures. Located in Los Angeles, it is the premier institution celebrating the art, science, and creators of cinema. As Chairman of the Museum's Board of Trustees, our very own Olivier de Givenchy, together with Malak Santini, Managing Director, were thrilled to represent the firm and attend the fourth annual gala, which raised funds for exhibitions and public programs serving the local community. As an official partner, we share in the Museum’s mission to provide broader access to artistic and educational resources and to promote diversity and inclusion across culture.

The film industry provides employment for millions of Americans, supports small businesses, and enhances the cultural fabric of communities worldwide. Fostering innovation and creativity, unlocking new technologies and bolstering job opportunities, both on screen and behind the screen, the industry produces a highly valued global export.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

#AcademyMuseum #TheAcademy

Credit: ©Academy Museum Foundation

The countdown to the end of the year begins. With 75 days left until 2025, 20 days until the next Federal Reserve meeting, and 18 days until the U.S. presidential election, now is as good a time as any to give your portfolio a tune-up. Here at #TopMarketTakeaways, we’re sharing three principles every investor should know, with added insights from our Chief Investment Officer, Richard Madigan. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Follow us for an exciting recap of our days in New York at Emerging Family Leaders, featuring dynamic networking, a tour of the iconic High Line, and valuable entrepreneurial insights into Latin America through engaging panels.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We are now two weeks into the fourth quarter. And so far, a solid economy has brought good returns year-to-date for global assets. The S&P 500 hit its 44th all-time high last week, while the MSCI World Index (a broad measure of global equities) is up roughly 19% year-to-date.

Can the rally continue? While past performance is no guarantee of future results, seasonality says yes:  The fourth quarter tends to provide a tailwind for markets. Seasonality in financial markets refers to patterns or trends that occur at specific times of the year, influencing the performance of various assets or sectors. These patterns can be driven by a range of factors, including economic cycles, investor behavior and cultural events. 

Historically, the fourth quarter has exhibited the best returns on average. Consumers spend more on retail during the holiday season, and the “Santa Claus” rally (where stocks tend to rise in the last week of December) and general optimism moving into the new year tend to benefit the calendar period. 

What’s more, when the first three quarters of the year post a positive return (as they did this year), the S&P 500 returns nearly 6% on average into year-end.

node.text: In honor of #HispanicHeritageMonth, we asked our colleagues what Hispanic and Latino Wealth Strategies means to them. Learn how we prioritize the goals and unique needs of our Hispanic and Latino clients.

Two weeks into the fourth quarter, a solid economy has brought good returns year-to-date for global assets. Can the rally continue? Historically, fourth quarters tend to exhibit the best returns on average, thanks in part to increased consumer spending and general optimism around the holidays. In #TopMarketTakeaways, we go beyond seasonal trends to examine how three global events could affect asset returns through the remainder of the year. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Past performance and outlooks are no guarantee of future results.

node.text: We’re back in the vibrant metropolis of Chicago for another edition of Emerging Family Leaders! Follow us for discussions on perseverance, AI, family business, and disrupting the norm and a little fun, too. #jpmorganprivatebank
In honor of #HispanicHeritageMonth, we celebrate the vibrant cultures, unique stories and significant contributions of our Hispanic and Latino colleagues throughout the firm.

Through the first half of this year, large caps carried the market and the S&P 500 returned more than 15%. Overall, the returns in the first half were top-heavy: Just four stocks (Nvidia, Microsoft, Alphabet and Amazon) contributed more than half of the S&P 500’s gain, while nearly 40% of the index was negative. What’s more, 126 companies performed better than the index itself. 
 
Performance has broadened out in the third quarter. Now 188 companies are performing better than the index, an increase of nearly 50% from the first half. Among those 188 companies, only three are in the “Magnificent 7”—compared to five in the first half of 2024. 
 
What does this mean for portfolios? It means that returns are getting less concentrated. Less concentrated markets create opportunities for active managers that have the skills to select outperformers and create alpha in portfolios.

Past performance is no guarantee of future results. It is not possible to invest directly in an index. All third-party companies referenced are shown for illustrative purposes only, and are not intended as a recommendation or endorsement by J.P. Morgan in this context.

The Federal Reserve meeting was the event of the year for economists and investors—but the news keeps rolling on. Over the past week at the “rate cut after-party,” a series of headlines, market moves and macroeconomic releases gave us confidence for the months ahead. Read on to learn more about the housing market, China’s policy announcements and the S&P 500’s all-time highs in #TopMarketTakeaways.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We’re proud to have been a sponsor of the 2024 L’ATTITUDE Conference in San Diego, celebrating the influence and innovation of the U.S. Latino community. Centered around the theme of “The New Mainstream Economy,” the event provided an inspiring opportunity to connect with visionary Latino leaders and innovators. Our Hispanic and Latino Wealth Strategies Team led a discussion on key economic trends and opportunities that featured Andrew Oshman, Ginger Chambless, Michael Harman and Claudio Robertson. Another highlight of the Conference for our clients was a sunset reception overlooking the San Diego skyline, which included remarks from Carlos Hernandez, Sol Trujillo and Gary Acosta. We remain committed to supporting the Latino community as it shapes the economy of tomorrow. #jpmorganprivatebank

Colleagues across J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management recently united for the annual Cycle for the Cause event organized by The LGBT Center in New York City. Riding more than 250 miles through Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut and New York, they raised over $150,000 to help the ongoing effort to combat HIV/AIDS and provide essential services such as testing, prevention education and support for those living with the disease.

On the heels of this week’s historic rate-cut decision, #TopMarketTakeaways zooms in on the potential market impact and how it might affect portfolios—especially your cash. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

This past weekend, team members from the Private Bank and Commercial Bank, Bambos Charalambous, Kirsty Hopkins, Jeff Schwartz, Marcus Vogel and Blake Rosenberg, participated in the Swim for Special Operation Forces hosted by the Honor Foundation. This was the fourth consecutive year JPMorganChase sponsored the event. The Honor Foundation, founded in 2014, assists in the transition of special operation forces from the military into jobs in the civilian sector. We believe in this important cause and we’ve hosted sessions at our One Paseo office, where fellows from the program can learn about our operations and what it’s like to work at JPMorganChase.

Produced in partnership with Beauhurst, this comprehensive analysis highlights the United Kingdom’s women-powered ecosystem. We proudly showcase the significant contributions of women investors and entrepreneurs nationwide.

Whether through compelling statistics or first-person insights, we hope these findings will drive meaningful progress towards a more equitable distribution of wealth and opportunity. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The debate is over. The Federal Reserve cut its policy rate by 50 basis points to 4.75%–5.0%, sparking a positive market reaction. Stocks hit an intra-day all-time high, and short-term bond yields fell. The Fed sees balanced inflation risks but rising unemployment risks. We agree. The labor market is slowing, but with the Fed easing policy to support growth, we believe it can extend the current economic cycle. Its decision to cut is as an acknowledgment that reinvestment risk is real—and bond yields could still move lower from here. It's worth noting that historically over the last 12 cutting cycles (from first cut to last cut), fixed income has outperformed cash in all but one. What’s more, when the Fed cuts rates outside of a recession, equities have typically delivered strong performance. 

Past performance is no guarantee of future results.

The yield curve is making waves. Last week, it continued to bull steepen, meaning that short-term yields dropped more than long-term ones. Here’s the scoop:

• 2-year yields fell by 22 basis points (the biggest drop since January)
• 10-year yields finished 18 basis points lower
• The 2s/10s curve is no longer inverted for the first time in 567 trading days

Why does this matter? When the yield curve inverts, it usually signals that banks might tighten lending standards, which results in investors becoming cautious. Historically, this has preceded five of the last six recessions. But this time, we think the uninversion suggests the market is confident that a rate-cutting cycle is just around the corner.

Futures markets are betting on a 100% chance of a 25-basis-point cut next week, with a 25% chance of a 50-basis-point cut. This bull-steepening trend can indicate optimism about future economic growth—think soft landing.

In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we investigate the “haves” and “have nots,” from AI-linked stocks to corporate credit, and share a few ways investors can create a buffer against short-term volatility.

Cultural icons Alicia Keys and Swizz Beatz sit down with Chelsea Crowder-Luke to discuss their book “Giants.” Featured on J.P. Morgan’s 2024 Summer Reading List, this collection highlights works by nearly 40 multigenerational Black American, African and Black diasporic artists. Learn more about the couple’s efforts to support diverse artists, their plans for a global museum tour and how the arts have helped them both realize their dreams.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

#jpmorganprivatebank

It’s time to put the white clothes away and get ready for back to school. If you were away for the summer, here’s what you missed:

Since Memorial Day, the S&P 500 has gained 6.8%, and only one sector (energy) had a negative return. The best-performing sectors over the period were real estate (+18%), financials (+11%) and healthcare (+11%). The NASDAQ 100 and Magnificent 7 underperformed over the period, returning 3.7% and 6.3%, respectively. Small caps (Solactive 2000 +7%) outperformed. Looking at just these numbers, one could reasonably assume it was an uneventful summer, but it wasn’t all smooth sailing. 

A currency trade unwind, unemployment rising—which triggered the Sahm rule—and the election getting closer all caused some summer volatility. Under the surface, the S&P 500, NASDAQ 100 and small caps experienced summer drawdowns of -8%, -13% and -9%, respectively.

As we head toward year-end, we expect policy rates to move lower, which should support the economy in navigating a soft landing.

This summer was filled with twists and turns. There was some whiplash—the S&P 500 hit its 38th all-time high in July before an -8.5% pullback in August—but stocks recovered quickly and now sit just 1% from the all-time highs. As we gear up for the fall, we’ve narrowed down the top three moments from August in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways: 
 
1. The unemployment rate rose
2. Investors did their homework, and bought the dip
3. The Federal Reserve signaled that cuts are here
 
Tap the link in bio to read on for our full discussion on what these could mean for investors.

Last week, the Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City hosted its annual Economic Policy Symposium in Jackson Hole, Wyoming. The event brings together economists, central bankers, academics, financial organizations and members of government to discuss policy issues.

All eyes and ears were on Fed Chair Powell’s Friday morning speech. Powell’s time at the podium made it clear that the Fed will be cutting interest rates come September. To use his words, “the time has come for policy to adjust,” and the committee does “not seek or welcome further cooling in labor market conditions.” The remarks reinforce what we’ve been saying: Rates are set to move lower from here, even if Powell didn’t tip his hand to reveal the magnitude of the cuts to come. 

In the 12 Fed cutting cycles, bonds outperformed cash 11 of those times. On average during those cutting cycles, bonds outperformed cash by 14%. What’s more, bonds had a positive return regardless of whether the Fed was cutting into a recession or a soft landing.

Our base case calls for three 25-basis-point cuts this year, with the first to come in September. Regardless of the pace of cuts, this is as good as it gets for cash yields—investors with excess cash should consider extending duration to lock in elevated yields for longer.

Our Women and Wealth Team hosted an exclusive luncheon featuring tennis great Lindsay Davenport. In a heartfelt conversation with Private Banker Kimberly Rose, Lindsay shared her incredible journey from the court to life beyond tennis. Her story of success and resilience set an inspiring tone as we celebrate our 43rd year as a sponsor of the US Open. 

#usopen #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text: We’ve been at the heart of the US Open for 43 years. From game-changing moments to Grand Slam victories, we’re here for every game, set and match, celebrating the spirit of the sport. #usopen #jpmorganprivatebank

After a summer of big market swings and uncertainties, it’s time for investors to renew their focus on what matters most. This week, #TopMarketTakeaways reviews some of the timeless keys to investing in any market. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

U.S. consumer sentiment rose for the first time in five months throughout August, according to the University of Michigan’s survey data. The boost stemmed from expectations for improved personal finances, more optimism about retirement income and better perceptions of long-term business conditions in the United States.

Given this week’s Democratic National Convention in Chicago, we examined the survey’s insights on sentiment among political parties. This was the first survey since President Biden dropped out of the 2024 election and Vice President Harris became the Democratic nominee.

Democrats saw a six-point boost in sentiment with Harris’s campaign, while Republicans reported a five-point decline, and Independents a three-point rise. When asked who was better for the economy, 41% chose Harris versus 38% for former President Trump, a shift from previous months that indicated a commanding lead for Trump. 

While politics can influence our perspectives, maintaining a long-term, goals-based approach to investing is crucial. Despite 18 presidential elections and 10 White House transitions since 1950, U.S. GDP growth has averaged 3.2% annually, and the S&P 500 has compounded at 9.4% per year. Focusing on fundamental market drivers over political changes can help us to benefit from long-term economic growth and market returns.

Bloomberg Finance, L.P. Data as of August 19, 2024.

The information presented is not intended to be making value judgments on the preferred outcome of any government decision or political election.

As markets rally and the Federal Reserve eyes a potential rate cut, it’s crucial to have your investment strategies in place. Consider defensive bond positions to offensive plays in rate-sensitive sectors, our #TopMarketTakeaways provides a comprehensive rate-cutting playbook with the guidance you need to navigate the upcoming market shifts. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
Disinflation is continuing, meaning that prices are increasing—but at a slower rate. The questions about the health of the labor market, catalyzed by last month’s jobs data, have firmly shifted the focus of the dual mandate on the labor side. That has taken some pressure off of this inflation print.

Nonetheless, today’s July Consumer Price Index data printed a 2.9% year-over-year increase, down from 3.0% in June. While above the Federal Reserve’s 2% target, it’s down from the 9.1% cycle peak and the 6.1% figure from one year ago. This is encouraging news for consumers and central bankers.

As inflation is no longer threatening, it gives central banks the flexibility to defend growth and labor markets. Today’s in-line print reinforces the broad trend that price increases are moving lower, and policy rates should follow. #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
As part of the 25th anniversary of our 2024 Summer Reading List, we are delighted to spotlight “The Secret Society of Aunts & Uncles,” a whimsical adventure story for children written by actor Jake Gyllenhaal. Jake recently spoke with Darin Oduyoye about the family members who inspire him and the creative process behind his first children’s book. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The market environment has changed. Stock prices and bond yields are down, while implied volatility is higher. We believe three main factors—a growth scare, AI skepticism and the upcoming U.S. election—drove investors to flee crowded positions. Are fluctuations a reason to sit still? Not in our view. In this week's #TopMarketTakeaways, we share seven steps investors can consider taking to make the most of market volatility. 

JPMorganChase, its affiliates, and employees do not provide tax, legal or accounting advice. You should consult your own tax, legal and accounting advisors before engaging in any financial transaction.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Volatility is back, and we acknowledge that it can be uncomfortable for investors. Following a battery of high-profile economic data releases last week, the risk pendulum has swung rapidly from sticky inflation concerns to impending recession. But we encourage investors to not miss the forest for the trees. The average year that ends with a gain in the market comes with an 11% peak-to-trough decline, and as of Monday, we are ~8% below all-time highs. 

Moves, both big, as in 2022’s rout (which was the worst for U.S. stocks since the Global Financial Crisis), and small, as we felt during March 2023’s bank stress, remind us that pullbacks are a feature, not a bug, of investing. The S&P 500 has suffered an average intra-year pullback of -14% over the past four decades, with 16 of those 44 years seeing even steeper losses. For investors, the “risk” in the stock market is volatility, while the “return” is the long-term capital appreciation that has historically rewarded investors.

We know there’s always something to worry about. Yet, principles like the ones above can be powerful tools to help avoid the pitfalls of emotionally driven, badly timed mistakes, as well as to help capitalize on opportunities aligned with your goals. #jpmorganprivatebank

We recently celebrated the Black Wealth Strategies Team at an exclusive reception on Martha’s Vineyard, hosted by Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon. This event was an example of JPMorganChase’s dedication to being the bank of choice for all by breaking down barriers, empowering communities and fostering financial prosperity for everyone.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

It’s been a turbulent week. Stocks saw big swings and bonds rallied amid U.S. election uncertainty, fears that the Federal Reserve is “behind the curve,” new earnings reports and other developments. It’s easy to get overwhelmed by market volatility, but it can be helpful to focus on the big picture. In the latest #TopMarketTakeaways, we’re sharing three lessons we’ve learned this week from the Fed, second-quarter “Magnificent 7” results and geopolitical headlines.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The Federal Reserve kept policy rates unchanged at 5.25–5.5% yesterday, marking over one year “on hold,” the third-longest period of unchanged rates since 1970.
 
Stock market performance during “holding patterns” tends to vary depending on whether the economy has a soft landing or a recession. Our view is that the economy will achieve a soft landing: Since the Fed went on hold, the S&P 500 has returned over 20%.
 
According to the Fed, its “dual mandate,” consisting of stable prices and low unemployment, is more in balance. As such, we think the next move for the Fed will be a rate cut in September. That should help alleviate some pressure on the economy, and continue its path toward a soft landing. #jpmorganprivatebank

Follow us in Singapore at our second annual Women’s Forum to celebrate “The Power of Perseverance.” At the Forum, we bring together entrepreneurs and members of the business community to promote the success and financial well-being of women across the region. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

#JPMPBWomensForum #jpmorganprivatebank

In the first half of the year, large-cap stocks carried the market. But the tables have now turned. Small-cap stocks have rallied over 9%, compared to just over 2% for the S&P 500, and less than 1% for the NASDAQ 100. Performance is broadening across the equity market—but why now? In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we assess the fundamentals, the technicals and the political landscape, and what it all means for investors.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The June inflation print sent a cool wave through the summer heat. 

Headline inflation was -0.1% month-over-month and negative for the first time since May 2020 (meaning prices actually decreased), and the core measure (ex-energy and food) was only 0.06%. Even some of the stickiest items, such as shelter costs, finally slowed. Core goods prices fell on the month. So did airfares and gasoline. A rate cut from the Federal Reserve now seems all but certain before year-end. In fact, markets are pricing in an almost 100% probability of a cut at the September meeting. 

The bottom line? Inflation is no longer threatening and a rate cut in 2024 is now all but certain, which should bode well both for the economy and the markets through the remainder of the year. #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
As part of J.P. Morgan’s Summer Reading List, acclaimed actor Matt Damon and water expert Gary White of @water sit down to discuss, “The Worth of Water,” which shares their mission to end the global water crisis. The book was selected as this year’s Anniversary Spotlight, highlighting the 225th anniversary of our earliest predecessor, a water works company founded in 1799.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

A wave of green has washed over markets this summer. The S&P 500 has made 13 all-time highs since Memorial Day, with 37 year-to-date. What’s been happening? Inflation is no longer threatening, growth has cooled, and the labor market has normalized—meaning that a rate cut from the Federal Reserve seems all but certain in the next six months. Since many investors are hesitant to get or stay invested, we’re sharing three lessons to help you embrace the rally in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

Tap the link in bio to learn more. 

Investing in equities involves risk. The price of equity securities may rise or fall due to the changes in the broad market or changes in a company's financial condition, sometimes rapidly or unpredictably. Equity securities are subject to "stock market risk" meaning that stock prices in general may decline over short or extended periods of time.

By sharing unique aspects of her Indian culture with others, Global Head of Digital, Data & Distribution Solutions, Mira Hole, believes it shapes who she is and helps bridge connections with others. Her daughter also enjoys expressing her heritage through the art of henna and its beautiful designs.
 
“Everybody’s looking for the same things – friendship, to belong, to express yourself, find your way – and you can share parts of a rich heritage that really make you unique and who you are,” said Mira.
 
Join us in honoring the unique traditions and values we each bring to our community. What traditions connect you with your roots?

At the Private Bank, we know that our clients’ identities and values shape their financial decisions. Diverse Wealth Strategies offer a full breadth of wealth management strategies informed by our understanding of who you truly are. From planning and investing to family governance and philanthropy, see how we can help you build and preserve your wealth with services tailored to your unique goals and needs.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Climate talk is heating up. In the past few weeks, Hurricane Beryl became the earliest Category 5 on record for the Atlantic Basin and the first of its size recorded in the month of June. To boot, a recent study suggested that future warming could be more severe than previously thought. While the news is concerning, within climate investing it’s not all doom and gloom. Significant investment in the sector (for every $1 invested in fossil fuels today, almost $2 are invested in clean energy*) has led to advancements. For example, the costs of solar panels, wind turbines and batteries have come down meaningfully since 2010. 
 
Climate challenges are growing, which has created opportunities to invest in sectors that are working to respond, including technology and infrastructure. While broad investment in the space has increased, private equity capital has lagged. Investors interested in this space may want to consider this "missing middle" and explore investing in companies leading the response to climate change and the energy transition which includes solar and wind power production and battery storage. #jpmorganprivatebank 

*Source: Scientific American, June 12, 2024
 
Investment in alternative investment strategies is speculative, often involves a greater degree of risk than traditional investments including limited liquidity and limited transparency, among other factors and should only be considered by sophisticated investors with the financial capability to accept the loss of all or part of the assets devoted to such strategies.

Set in the Swedish Riviera, we were thrilled to partner with The Aurora in its inaugural year. A mesmerising experience for car enthusiasts, the three-day event showcased state-of-the-art hypercars and timeless, classic models from Koenigsegg and MOTIKON. 
 
To kick off the weekend, J.P. Morgan Private Bank hosted an exclusive cocktail and terrace dinner, where attendees shared their passion for cars and enjoyed a taste of the Scandinavian lifestyle. 

To learn more visit the link in bio.

The first six months of 2024 were the fifth best for the S&P 500 in the last 25 years, with just four stocks (Nvidia, Microsoft, Alphabet and Amazon) contributing more than half of the gain. Things are looking up: Historically, first strong halves tend to beget strong second halves, and the inflation outlook has been beneficial to the stock market. Before heading into the holiday weekend, we take a timeout to explore the events that have dominated the headlines so far this year in #TopMarketTakeaways. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more. 

Past performance is no guarantee of future results.

Managing Director, Market Team Lead, David Garcia and his team are passionate about delivering world-class banking services and access to financial advice from around the globe to their Private Bank clients in Las Vegas. Whether you’re focused on building, preserving or transferring wealth, you can benefit from our global network and insights, tailored to help you achieve your financial goals.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.
In celebration of #PrideMonth, we recognize and celebrate the diverse cultures, stories and contributions of our #LGBTQ+ colleagues across the firm.

This Pride Month, LGBTQ+ Wealth Strategies proudly hosted events across Dallas, Los Angeles, New York, San Francisco and Washington, DC, to celebrate the LGBTQ+ community. These gatherings sparked engaging conversations about the lived experiences of community members. Thank you for joining us in support of our mission to champion equity and inclusion.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The Congressional Budget Office recently projected a $2 trillion deficit in the U.S. federal budget this year, as well as a highest-ever debt-to-GDP ratio of 120% by 2034. While financial markets and presidential candidates have shown indifference, many investors are rightly concerned about the underlying risks associated with wide deficits and high sovereign debt levels. Read this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways for a discussion of five common concerns, and what they could mean for your portfolio.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

As we close out June, we thought it apt to check in on the economy and markets. The bottom line? As we cover in our #MidYearOutlook, growth is strong but cooling, inflation has continued to progress, and the Federal Reserve looks well positioned to deliver a rate cut before the end of the year. 

When it comes to markets, investors seem to have the luxury of choice with elevated rates across the fixed income spectrum, while earnings growth has continued to push stocks higher. 

The vital signs for the markets and the macro are trending in the right direction despite the risks posed by fragilities such as the U.S. election and geopolitical events. We encourage investors to perform their own checkups to ensure their portfolios are aligned with their long-term plans

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

#jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
Entrepreneur and bestselling author Fawn Weaver’s newly released book “Love & Whiskey” is a highlight of our 2024 Summer Reading List. Fawn, founder of the billion-dollar brand Uncle Nearest Premium Whiskey, tells the remarkable true story of her company’s namesake, Nearest Green, a formerly enslaved African American master distiller who played a key role in developing Jack Daniel’s Tennessee whiskey. As we celebrate Juneteenth, watch Fawn’s discussion with Debra Langford, Head of Black Wealth Strategies for J.P. Morgan Private Bank, about this inspiring figure.

Tap the link in bio to watch.

#jpmorganprivatebank

This week’s macro mania gave us confidence in our #MidYearOutlook. Stocks have been hitting new highs and bonds are rallying, while disinflation is sending a cool wave through the summer heat. Genuine risks around elections and geopolitical turmoil persist, but the global economy is strong. How can investors respond? In #TopMarketTakeaways, we discuss five actions worth considering in your portfolio today.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Bonds are subject to interest rate risk, credit and default risk of the issuer. Bond prices generally fall when interest rates rise. The price of equity securities may rise or fall due to the changes in the broad market or changes in a company's financial condition, sometimes rapidly or unpredictably. Equity securities are subject to "stock market risk" meaning that stock prices in general may decline over short or extended periods of time.

It’s official: More central banks are cutting rates than hiking them. However, the Federal Reserve is not joining in on the party just yet. It held rates steady at its Wednesday meeting following a softer-than-expected May CPI report, an encouraging step forward in the inflation narrative.

That said, the macro-mania has not shifted our expectations, and we still think the Fed’s next move will be a rate cut—and markets agree. But it will likely take continued supportive economic data and patience, as we do not expect the move until December of this year. 

Zooming out, today’s higher growth, moderate inflation, gradual easing amid strong corporate earnings, and AI optimism has left us increasingly constructive about the road ahead. #jpmorganprivatebank

At J.P. Morgan Private Bank, we recognize that your wealth journey is deeply personal. With Diverse Wealth Strategies, we provide customized advice tailored to the unique goals and needs of our diverse clients, including women, Asian, Black, Hispanic and Latino, and LGBTQ+ communities. We don’t just manage wealth, we understand the values and cultural dynamics that shape your financial decisions. 

To experience the difference tap the link in bio.

Newly released: Mid-Year Outlook 2024. A strong economy in a fragile world.  The economy is stronger than you think. Yes, geopolitical risk is real. But stocks can move higher as markets find renewed momentum in a robust global economy. Are you embracing the rally? 

Tap the link in bio to read our #MidYearOutlook2024
